name: Dynamic Field of View
description: A field-of-view adjustment that responds to speed, sprinting, boosting, aiming, or other gameplay states.
design_notes: Dynamic FOV is a motion cue, not a physics change. It can make sprinting, boosting, falling, or speed pickups feel faster, but it must not hide threats or cause discomfort. Treat the effect as a camera profile with caps, blend rules, and reduced-motion scaling.
design_purpose: Communicate acceleration, sprint, boost, or high-speed traversal through the viewpoint.
Make state changes feel responsive without changing movement numbers.
Support weapon, vehicle, and traversal profiles with distinct presentation.
player_fantasy: The player feels a surge of momentum when the viewpoint opens up during fast movement.

parameters.name: base_fov_degrees
parameters.description: Default field of view before dynamic offsets.

parameters.name: sprint_fov_add_degrees
parameters.description: FOV increase applied during sprint or equivalent high-speed state.

parameters.name: boost_fov_add_degrees
parameters.description: Additional FOV increase for temporary boosts or vehicle bursts.

parameters.name: fov_blend_ms
parameters.description: Blend window used when entering or leaving a FOV profile.

parameters.name: reduced_motion_fov_scale
parameters.description: Player setting multiplier applied to dynamic FOV offsets.

edge_cases: ADS, scopes, cutscenes, vehicles, and sprint can request conflicting FOV profiles; priority must be deterministic.
High FOV on ultrawide displays can distort reticles and weapon models if presentation is not separated.
Rapidly entering and leaving sprint can create pulsing unless blends are debounced.
Network correction should not replay FOV pulses every time speed is reconciled.
common_bugs: FOV offset is added repeatedly every frame instead of blended from a base value.
Reduced-motion settings scale camera shake but not dynamic FOV.
Weapon viewmodel FOV and world camera FOV desynchronize during sprint transitions.
FOV never resets after death or a cinematic camera overrides the player camera.
balancing_notes: Dynamic FOV can make movement feel faster than it is; tune speed perception alongside actual movement values.
Large FOV shifts should be reserved for strong state changes such as boost, not every small velocity fluctuation.
accessibility_notes: Expose a dynamic-FOV intensity slider and allow disabling the effect without changing gameplay speed.
Avoid stacking dynamic FOV with screen shake and camera roll unless reduced-motion settings scale all of them together.

implementation_notes.unity: Compute a target FOV from the active camera profile stack, including sprint and boost offsets, then interpolate Cinemachine lens values from a stable base FOV.
implementation_notes.godot: Drive Camera3D.fov from movement-state profiles in _process and keep the base user FOV separate from temporary offsets.
implementation_notes.unreal: Blend FOV through PlayerCameraManager or camera modifiers, with aiming and sprint states contributing named modifiers rather than writing directly to the camera.
implementation_notes.web: For Three.js, update camera.fov from base plus active offsets and call updateProjectionMatrix; for 2D cameras, use a separate zoom profile if FOV is not applicable.

example_games.title: Minecraft
example_games.note: Uses FOV changes when sprinting or speed effects are active.

example_games.title: Titanfall 2
example_games.note: Uses speed-oriented camera presentation to support high-mobility traversal.
